The academic year is divided into winter semester and summer semester. Each semester consists of a period during which courses are held and a lecuture-free period ("holidays").

- The University of Vienna is closed on [public holidays](https://www.wien.gv.at/amtshelfer/feiertage/).

retrieved.text: / - c.t. (cum tempore) If you find the indication "cum tempore" (c.t.) next to a course in the course directory, the course starts 15 minutes later than scheduled. These fifteen minutes are referred to as "academic quarter of an hour" or "academic quarter" (in German: „akademische Viertelstunde“ or „akademisches Viertel“). Example: Laura would like to attend the lecture „Höhere Analysis“. According to [u:find](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/abc-of-terminology/#c273318), it starts at 10:00 c.t. In order to be punctual, Laura must arrive at the lecture hall no later than 10:15. retrieved.title: Application and admission periods
retrieved.text:
[### Attention: changes for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure for the academic year 2026/27 (please click here)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/)

On this site you will find an overview of the application and admission periods for bachelor/diploma and master programmes at the University of Vienna.

- You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period)](#c955669).
- You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (= registration period for the continuation of studies)](#c955681).

The winter semester always starts on 1 October, the summer semester on 1 March.
[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

- The application for degree programmes with entrance exam or selection procedure is only possible once per academic year!
  - [Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)
  - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)
- Separate application and admission periods apply for admission to [non-degree programmes](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/).
- If you want to attend the [Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) you also have to keep the registration periods of the VWU in mind.

retrieved.title: Planning your semester
retrieved.text: for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively, you can also use the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass) which outlines the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which achievements you have already completed and which ones you have to complete next. In the [course directory u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/index.html), you can select the specific courses for the current semester. Factors, such as content, time, venue, lecturer, etc., may help you in your decision. The workload required to complete a course is indicated in the form of ECTS credits. On average, completing a degree programme within its standard duration takes 30 ECTS credits per semester. Please note that you have to complete an [introductory and orientation period (STEOP)](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/) at the beginning of every bachelor’s programme before you are allowed to register for any other courses. In addition to compulsory and elective modules, you will have to complete [extension curricula](/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/) (EC) in almost all bachelor’s programmes. You can register for EC in u:space. retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: the course. For courses with continuous assessment the allocation process begins after the registration period has ended. Registrations are ranked according to the stipulations in the curricula (e.g. point system/preference system/study achievement system) and places allocated. Afterwards, you will be informed about whether you are “Registered” (Angemeldet), "On waiting list" (auf Warteliste) or if there is an "Alternative place available" (Alternativplatz verfügbar). If students deregister from a course, you possibly move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. If an Alternative place is available, you can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. You can find detailed information about the registration procedure at the website of the course and exam registration system. For further information about the registration for courses/exams, please contact the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS)/StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) of your degree programme. reranked.title: Application and admission periods
reranked.text:
[### Attention: changes for bachelor and diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure for the academic year 2026/27 (please click here)](https://aufnahmeverfahren.univie.ac.at/en/)

On this site you will find an overview of the application and admission periods for bachelor/diploma and master programmes at the University of Vienna.

- You have to submit your application for admission to a degree programme within the [application period (= general admission period)](#c955669).
- You can complete the admission procedure within the [admission period (= registration period for the continuation of studies)](#c955681).

The winter semester always starts on 1 October, the summer semester on 1 March.
[Schedule of the academic year](/en/semester-planning/the-academic-year/)

- The application for degree programmes with entrance exam or selection procedure is only possible once per academic year!
  - [Bachelor/diploma programmes with entrance exam procedure](/en/entrance-exam/)
  - [Master programmes with selection procedure](/en/admission-procedure/master-programmes/#c876069)
- Separate application and admission periods apply for admission to [non-degree programmes](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/).
- If you want to attend the [Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU)](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/university-preparation-programme-of-the-vienna-universities-vwu/) you also have to keep the registration periods of the VWU in mind.

reranked.title: Planning your semester
reranked.text: for the current semester and create your personal schedule. In the semester planner, you will find a calendar that shows all planned courses and exams for your degree programmes. This allows you to quickly find and resolve overlapping dates. Alternatively, you can also use the [record of examinations in u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/pruefungspass) which outlines the structure of your curriculum. This way, you can see at a glance which achievements you have already completed and which ones you have to complete next. In the [course directory u:find](https://ufind.univie.ac.at/de/index.html), you can select the specific courses for the current semester. Factors, such as content, time, venue, lecturer, etc., may help you in your decision. The workload required to complete a course is indicated in the form of ECTS credits. On average, completing a degree programme within its standard duration takes 30 ECTS credits per semester. Please note that you have to complete an [introductory and orientation period (STEOP)](/en/semester-planning/introductory-and-orientation-period-steop/) at the beginning of every bachelor’s programme before you are allowed to register for any other courses. In addition to compulsory and elective modules, you will have to complete [extension curricula](/en/extension-curricula-and-alternative-extensions/) (EC) in almost all bachelor’s programmes. You can register for EC in u:space. Tips for semester planning in u:find The course directory u:find offers three help tools for semester planning, which are still known to only a few students: - In the overview of all courses in

reranked.text: the course. For courses with continuous assessment the allocation process begins after the registration period has ended. Registrations are ranked according to the stipulations in the curricula (e.g. point system/preference system/study achievement system) and places allocated. Afterwards, you will be informed about whether you are “Registered” (Angemeldet), "On waiting list" (auf Warteliste) or if there is an "Alternative place available" (Alternativplatz verfügbar). If students deregister from a course, you possibly move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. If an Alternative place is available, you can accept or reject it within a defined deadline. You can find detailed information about the registration procedure at the website of the course and exam registration system.
